name: Crop animation - optimistic grow transition
description:
Smooth animation when crop is applied.

Goal: Make crop feel instant and fluid.

Idea:
- When user applies crop, animate the crop area growing to fill the video display
- Do this optimistically (snapshot the cropped region, animate it) while the real FFmpeg crop runs in background
- Once backend finishes, swap in the real result seamlessly

This is about perceived performance and polish, not just visual flair.

name: Playhead stability during video operations
description:
Keep playhead position stable across edits to reduce jarring resets.

Goals:
- After crop: stay on the same frame (not reset to start)
- After cut: intelligent snap behavior
  - If playhead inside cut area: snap to start of cut
  - If playhead after cut: adjust by cut duration
  - If playhead before cut: no change needed

This is about preserving user context during editing operations.
result:
Implemented playhead stability with backend position tracking:

**Backend (bridge.py):**
- Added `suggestedPositionMs` property to SessionBridge
- `applyCrop`: preserves current position (same frame)
- `applyCut`: intelligent adjustment logic
  - Position before cut: unchanged
  - Position inside cut: snap to cut start
  - Position after cut: shift by cut duration
- `undo/redo`: preserve current position (QML clamps to duration)

**QML (Main.qml):**
- Pass current position to all operations
- Use `suggestedPositionMs` in onVideoChanged
- Clamp to video duration for edge cases

**Tests:**
- 7 new tests for position tracking
- 154 total tests passing

commit.message: Add playhead stability across video operations

name: Cut animation - timeline collapse transition
description:
Smooth animation when a segment is cut from the timeline.

Goal: The timeline should visually collapse around the removed segment.

Challenges:
- Thumbnails change as the video shortens
- Need to be smart about performance
- Options: visual scale then redraw, or real-time thumbnail updates during transition

Ideal: Update thumbnails at ~30-60fps during a fast collapse animation. Can be economical - just enough frames to feel good without overdoing performance.

This is about making cuts feel fluid and maintaining spatial context.

name: Frame-accurate editing precision
description:
Consider moving from millisecond-based to frame-based precision for professional editing.

**Current state:**
- Playhead position in milliseconds (Qt Video player default)
- Markers stored as float seconds
- FFmpeg handles frame boundaries on actual cuts
- Good enough for casual editing

**Frame-accurate would involve:**
- Probe video framerate on load (store in session)
- Timeline quantizes positions to frame boundaries
- Markers snap to exact frames
- Timecode display: HH:MM:SS:FF instead of MM:SS
- Arrow keys move by single frames
- Scrubbing snaps to frames

**Benefits:**
- Professional-grade precision
- No ambiguity about cut points
- Matches industry-standard NLE behavior

**Questions to answer after more use:**
- Is ms precision causing real problems?
- Do cuts land on unexpected frames?
- Is timecode display needed for the workflow?

Revisit after using the editor more to see if this precision matters in practice.

name: Make markers selectable
description:
## What
Add selection state to cut bar markers, allowing users to select markers for keyboard-based operations.

## Why
Currently markers can only be dragged with the mouse. Selection enables:
- Keyboard-based precision positioning (arrow keys)
- Delete key to remove markers
- Better accessibility

## Implementation Approach

### Visual states
- **Default**: Current marker appearance (flag-style handle)
- **Hover**: Faint thick border around the marker handle
- **Selected**: Highlighted border of same thickness (more prominent)

### Cursor
- Use normal arrow cursor (no special cursor on hover)

### Interactions
- Click marker to select it
- Click elsewhere to deselect
- Only one marker selected at a time

### Keyboard support (when selected)
- Left/Right arrows: Move marker by small increment
- Shift+Left/Right: Move by larger increment
- Delete/Backspace: Remove the selected marker

### Backend
- Selection is UI-only state (not persisted to session/undo)
- Move/delete operations use existing backend methods

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hover shows faint border on marker handle
- [ ] Click selects marker with highlighted border
- [ ] Click elsewhere deselects
- [ ] Arrow keys move selected marker
- [ ] Delete removes selected marker
- [ ] Tests for new interactions
result:
Implemented marker selection in CutBar.qml:
- Added selectedMarkerTime property (-1 = no selection)
- Visual states: hover (faint border), selected (highlighted border)
- Click marker to select, click elsewhere to deselect
- Keyboard support: Arrow keys move marker (small/large increment with Shift), Delete/Backspace removes
- Selection auto-clears when marker is removed externally
- Added 9 new tests covering all interactions
- All 163 tests pass

name: Cut bar - marker-based cutting experience
description:
Redesign cutting to use a dedicated cut bar with markers.

## Vision
A separate bar (above or below the timeline scrubber) for placing cut markers and removing segments. Same width as scrubber, represents video time. Scissor icon on the left to indicate purpose.

## Core interactions

**Placing markers:**
- Click on the cut bar to place a cut marker at that frame
- Multiple markers divide the video into segments

**Removing segments:**
- Hover over a segment (between markers) → highlights the section
- Hold modifier (⌘/Ctrl) → shows scissor icon in the segment
- Click while holding modifier → removes that segment
- Status bar hint: "Hold ⌘ and click to cut"

## Unified trim/cut model
This approach unifies trimming and cutting:
- Trim: place markers at trim points, delete outside segments
- Cut: place markers around unwanted section, delete that section
- Optional inverse modifier: "keep this segment, delete others"

## Goals
- Precise marker placement on exact frames
- Quick segment removal (mark mark mark, chop chop chop)
- One mode of interaction for both trim and edit operations
- Flexibility for complex edits

Note: This replaces the current Shift+drag selection approach with a marker-based model.
result:
Implemented marker-based cut bar for segment removal.

## What was built

**CutBar.qml** - New UI component above the timeline:
- Scissor icon on the left to indicate purpose
- Click anywhere to place a cut marker at that frame
- Hover over segments to see highlighting
- ⌘/Ctrl+click on a segment to remove it
- Status hint appears on hover: 'Hold ⌘ and click to cut'

**Backend marker model** in EditorSession:
- Markers are first-class concepts with full undo/redo
- SessionSnapshot now includes both video state and markers
- add_marker(), remove_marker(), clear_markers() operations
- apply_cut() adjusts markers when cuts are made:
  - Markers before cut region: unchanged
  - Markers inside cut region: removed
  - Markers after cut region: shifted earlier by cut duration

**Bridge updates**:
- markers property exposed to QML
- markersChanged signal for reactive UI updates
- addMarker/removeMarker/clearMarkers slots
- applyCut now uses session.apply_cut() for marker adjustment

**Cleanup**:
- Removed old Shift+drag selection from Timeline.qml
- Updated GUI tests for new CutBar behavior

## Verification
- 143 tests pass (38 new tests for markers)
- Lint passes (ruff check)
- Pre-commit hooks pass

## Not implemented (future)
- Inverse modifier ('keep this segment, delete others')
- Keyboard shortcuts for marker operations

commit.message: Add marker-based cut bar for segment removal

name: Keyboard and precision interactions
description:
Refine the editor with high-value precision interactions.

Ideas:
- Keyboard nudging for crop or markers
- Keyboard shortcuts for add cut, undo, redo, export
- Snapping or precision aids for marker placement

name: Add frame preview tooltip when dragging markers
description:
## What
Show a small video frame preview above the marker while dragging it, to help with precise positioning.

## Why
When dragging a marker to a specific frame, visual feedback of the actual frame helps users place markers precisely at scene boundaries or specific moments.

## Challenges
- Qt's Video/MediaPlayer seeking is not instantaneous
- Real-time frame updates during drag require smooth seeking performance
- May need alternative approaches:
  - Pre-extracted thumbnail strip with interpolation
  - GPU-accelerated frame extraction
  - Lower-resolution preview for speed
  - Debounced updates during fast dragging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Frame preview appears above marker during drag
- [ ] Preview updates smoothly as marker moves
- [ ] Shows current frame at marker position
- [ ] Displays timestamp
- [ ] Disappears when drag ends

name: Export the current working video
description:
Implement export as a separate save flow.

- Save dialog to choose destination
- Export operates on current working video (not directly on edit tools)
- Progress and error reporting
- Cancellation if practical

name: Loading states and progress indicators
description:
Show progress feedback during longer operations.

Goals:
- Spinner or progress bar during crop/cut/export
- Block UI appropriately while processing
- Clear visual indication that something is happening

Independent from animation work - can be done before or after.

name: End-to-end happy-path tests
description:
Small set of E2E tests exercising UI and backend together.

Candidate flows:
- open video -> crop -> apply -> export
- open video -> select segment -> cut -> export
- open video -> edit -> undo -> redo
